clean_body: Honoring the lives lost in 2001 and holding our memories close. | | | = Friend, Last month, Today is September 11, and it is so important to remember and honor the lives that were lost that sad day in 2001. Few events have tested the soul and spirit of our nation more than the attack on the Twin Towers and the Pentagon, as well as the loss of the passengers aboard Flight 93. Like most people old enough to comprehend what was happening on September 11, 2001, I remember where I was and what I was feeling when I heard about the plane flying into the first tower. I was in Boston - the location where the hijackers flew out of. I recall distinctly feeling a sense of dread and dark uncertainty upon learning that what we had witnessed was a deliberate attack. Were places like New York and Boston being targeted? Were we next? What was the motive for the attacks? It is easy to forget the feelings that persisted after 9/11 as well—the widespread fear and panic, not knowing what was safe, or how we could even begin picking up the pieces after the destructive evil we had just witnessed. Understanding just how resilient and remarkable the American spirit truly is requires looking at the terrible events of 9/11 and their aftermath with fresh eyes. Unprecedented and world-changing events shake up expectations, rattle the foundations of society, and present completely novel challenges no one could have predicted, or prepared for. In time, after the towers fell and the wreckage was surveyed from the Pentagon, it became clear that the people would not break—we hardened our resolve. We learned that passengers on Flight 93 courageously rose up against their captors and made the ultimate sacrifice to prevent it from reaching its target: Washington, D.C. Since that day, we have faced more unprecedented challenges including a violent insurrection directed at the seat of democratic power, the U.S. Congress. Defeating all forms of terrorism, foreign and domestic, is beyond important to my campaign which is why I have met with some of the nations foremost national security experts to formulate my policy on counter-terrorism and protecting America from attacks like the one that occured 22 years ago today and the one that occured on January 6 2021. And even now, I am moved by how Americans tend to come together during these times rather than split apart. Reflecting on the legacy of 9/11 makes me proud to be an American and to play my own small part in our incredible democratic experiment. We can withstand anything if we stand together. In Solidarity, Tamie | | | | | | Tamie Wilson Candidate for Congress (OH-4) | | | = To contribute via check, please address to: 273 SARATOGA ST. DELAWARE, OH 43015 you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please | |

name: JusticeDemocrats.com
subject: Doubling down
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

Over the last few years, with minimal resources compared to the Democratic
establishment, we have punched far above our weight and made an impact by
recruiting and electing an entire bloc of working-class leaders to
Congress. But we’re at a crossroads for our movement.

In a HuffPost article about our organization's 2023 layoffs, a progressive
senior House aide said:

“Without a threat of a primary and a weakening of Justice Democrats, we
go back to corporate interests and a lack of accountability from the
progressive movement.”

We cannot go back. Despite an unprecedented slowdown in our fundraising
over the last year and the need to lay off some of our incredible members
of staff as a result, we are not giving up or letting the corporate
establishment win.

We are doubling down on candidate recruitment and reviewing every viable
district on the map. We are building coalitions to ensure that our
incumbents can defend themselves against the multimillion-dollar threat of
AIPAC and right-wing donors. We are not stopping this crucial work — even
for a moment.

But we need your help to overcome this difficult time,
Peter.

Thank you so much for being a part of our movement to
challenge the entire political establishment and elect more progressive
changemakers.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/137952?refcode=em20230911-30805&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1002&akid=30805%2E1203867%2EeJZpdK ]Will you support us by making your first contribution of $3 right now
to fund our critical work?

Thanks again for your support,
Peter. Together we will
continue to fight for the soul of the Democratic Party.

In solidarity,

Justice Democrats

Do not worry if you cannot afford to make a contribution — we understand that
this is a difficult time. If you’re struggling, you can find a food bank
[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/28883?t=1003&akid=30805%2E1203867%2EeJZpdK ]here. We appreciate everything you do to keep our movement strong.

Please stay informed and follow the most up-to-date recommendations from the
[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/24438?t=1004&akid=30805%2E1203867%2EeJZpdK ]Centers for Disease Control and your state public health department.

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20230911-30805-footer&t=1005&refcode2=30805_1203867_eJZpdK&akid=30805%2E1203867%2EeJZpdK

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1006&akid=30805%2E1203867%2EeJZpdK

PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.
Justice Democrats
1390 Columbia Ave #261
Lancaster PA 17603
Email us: us@justicedemocrats.com

I’m proud to lend my support to Sylvia’s campaign. **She’s the first out trans woman to run for state office in Alabama.**
Sylvia got involved in service at the University of Alabama, participating in Students for Fair Labor. She never saw herself as a politician. But, this legislative session, Alabama passed several anti-trans pieces of legislation. **And when she went to call her representative to oppose the bills, she learned he’d resigned.**
**If elected, Sylvia would focus on:**
* Expanding Medicaid, bringing _free federal dollars_ to Alabama
* Improving wages to attract and retain educators
* “Banning the box” so that formerly incarcerated people can’t be denied job opportunities because of their past
* Advocating for making our democracy stronger and more inclusive by expanding mail in voting, automatic registration, and early voting
* Investing in public transportation

As a trans state legislator myself, I know how important — and difficult — it is for trans folks to step up and run for office. We need more representation in our government, not just because we’re trans, but because the issues important to our community are important to everyone. Getting a job. Renting an apartment. Having a family. Going to college.
**And our government works so much better when there are more voices at the table.**
[**So, if you’re able, make a donation NOW* — your contribution will be split between her campaign and mine. Sylvia's election is on September 26, and I hope you can show her the same support you’ve shown me throughout the years.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69575658/426570161/619169553?refcode=EM_FL_MCB_20230911_ta_swayne_cand_nc_full_xx_l2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NjI0OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZGQxOWUzNC1lNTUwLWVlMTEtYTNmMS0wMDIyNDgzMmUxYmEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=gGNC_VoiAzmU7t3u_3uRsNhkzzC6N3ZK24WE5FPOaeg=)

clean_body: | = I wanted to tell you about an important election coming up on September 26. But before I get too much further, I have to ask: = | = I’m proud to lend my support to Sylvia’s campaign. She’s the first out trans woman to run for state office in Alabama. Sylvia got involved in service at the University of Alabama, participating in Students for Fair Labor. She never saw herself as a politician. But, this legislative session, Alabama passed several anti-trans pieces of legislation. And when she went to call her representative to oppose the bills, she learned he’d resigned. If elected, Sylvia would focus on: Expanding Medicaid, bringing free federal dollars to Alabama Improving wages to attract and retain educators “Banning the box” so that formerly incarcerated people can’t be denied job opportunities because of their past Advocating for making our democracy stronger and more inclusive by expanding mail in voting, automatic registration, and early voting Investing in public transportation As a trans state legislator myself, I know how important — and difficult — it is for trans folks to step up and run for office. We need more representation in our government, not just because we’re trans, but because the issues important to our community are important to everyone. Getting a job. Renting an apartment. Having a family. Going to college. And our government works so much better when there are more voices at the table. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = | = | = ---|---|--- = | = | = Let’s go! \- Sarah Seriously, Sylvia needs your help right now because there are just 15 days until this election! Hi Peter,
Allow me to introduce myself! My name is Natalie Jones and I am the new Digital and Research Associate for Freedom Virginia. I’m a recent Virginia Commonwealth University graduate and a proud Hampton Roads resident. I have followed Freedom Virginia for a while and I'm so excited that I get to join them in the work they do for my fellow Virginians!
Some of you may have noticed our name hasn't popped up in your inbox for a little while. I’m excited to get our grassroots fundraising back up and running!
Now, I am asking for your help. We haven't been able to fundraise via email for over a month. The donations we receive through emails are crucial to our fight for Virginia. Just $5 will help us continue our efforts to make Virginia better for families. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/freedom-virginia]

name: Friends of the Earth
subject: Your Signature is Needed: Baby sea turtles are CHOKING on plastic
body:
| | | | | [](https://us.engagingnetworks.app/page/email/click/10012/1978157?email=RepJ2ZGQKX6xm5Aey7FwClESkN1MQhKC&campid=UI6tX83ZqbKZkArzVWMSmA==)
---|---|---

| | Between the months of May and October, baby sea turtles hatch from their tiny eggshells, opening their eyes to glimmering beach shores. Their small heads and flippers break through, hungry and eager to fill their stomachs with yummy jellyfish and seaweed. Hundreds of baby sea turtles across the country totter through hills of sand to make it to the big blue ocean. _But unfortunately, Friend, not all of them will make it..._ These small curious creatures are up against various predators, but there is one predator that is becoming invasive in the marine ecosystem— _**PLASTIC!**_ It is so easy for an innocent baby sea turtle to mistake plastic waste and scraps as food -- they can’t tell the difference. **ONE** single piece of plastic is **ENOUGH** to **_KILL_** a baby sea turtle. The plastic can get lodged in their tiny throats which can cause them to CHOKE and SUFFOCATE. Friend, our beach shores and oceans should be maintained and protected to support life, **_not destroy it._** **If we do not demand an end to this now, there could be more at stake.** **We want to ask YOU Friend: are you concerned that our oceans and planet could be at risk of being DESTROYED by plastic pollution without any urgent intervention?**
| | [YES](https://us.engagingnetworks.app/page/email/click/10012/1978158?email=RepJ2ZGQKX6xm5Aey7FwClESkN1MQhKC&campid=UI6tX83ZqbKZkArzVWMSmA==)
---
| [NO](https://us.engagingnetworks.app/page/email/click/10012/1978158?email=RepJ2ZGQKX6xm5Aey7FwClESkN1MQhKC&campid=UI6tX83ZqbKZkArzVWMSmA==)
---
It’s not just baby sea turtles at risk -- **people are too!** You do not need to be near a beach or the ocean to be impacted by Big Plastic’s and Big Polluter’s dirty choices. Big Plastic most often operates their industrial-sized factories in the backyards of _**low-income communities and communities of color.**_ Nearby residents are subjected to **inhaling harmful emissions** from their **toxic cancer-causing pollutants.**
As the production of plastic continues, the plastic that currently exists will **_NEVER DISAPPEAR._** _Where will all this plastic go, Friend, if not in overwhelming wastelands?_ Microplastic is even being discovered _**in our food, water sources, and the air that we breathe!**_ This is scary Friend -- plastic is invading our ecosystems, our bodies and potentially the future of our planet.
_**Do you think the Biden Administration should implement IMMEDIATE federal solutions to save people and the planet from the plastic crisis?**_
| | [YES](https://us.engagingnetworks.app/page/email/click/10012/1978158?email=RepJ2ZGQKX6xm5Aey7FwClESkN1MQhKC&campid=UI6tX83ZqbKZkArzVWMSmA==)
---
| [NO](https://us.engagingnetworks.app/page/email/click/10012/1978158?email=RepJ2ZGQKX6xm5Aey7FwClESkN1MQhKC&campid=UI6tX83ZqbKZkArzVWMSmA==)
---
Together, we can stop plastic pollution and save the planet.
Friends of the Earth
|

name: Jackson Lahmeyer
subject: Lara Trump & Kash Patel In Tulsarusalem!
body:
Lara Trump & Kash Patel @Sheridan.Church Last night was the kickoff to IGNITE 2023 at Sheridan.Church in Tulsa, OK. We were honored to have State Su
| | |
---
## Lara Trump & Kash Patel @Sheridan.Church
---
| [ ](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=1336955.574.1.88a1a02fc9af184d7e4768515a7fb87f)
---
Last night was the kickoff to [IGNITE 2023](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=1336955.575.1.cb499da65551d08876a62a09b7ebc27f) at [Sheridan.Church](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=1336955.576.1.32f7929376c16055e08b348bfb6d663c) in Tulsa, OK.
We were honored to have **State Superintendent Ryan Walters** , **Tulsa County Sheriff Vic Regalado** and **Tulsa City Councilor Jayme Fowler** share a few remarks about what is happening locally.
We also heard from **Kash Patel** & **Lara Trump** about what is taking place throughout our country.
One of the highlights was when **Lara Trump** called her _father-in-law_ , **President Donald Trump**!
**President Trump** spoke to the audience for several minutes and the atmosphere was electric.
ICYMI, watch Night 1 here: [https://rumble.com/v3ge5k3-lara-trump-and-kash-patel-live-from-tulsarusalem.html](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=1336955.577.1.306b86693330c91e42e7a67591174fb1)
Tonight is Night 2 and we have Evangelist David Ring (born with cerebral palsy) preaching at 7pm.
| | [ Watch: Lara Trump & Kash Patel LIVE ](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=1336955.578.1.d5220326e66934cfd2b4372d8297e60b)
---
---
## IGNITE 2023 | Sheridan.Church
---
| [ ](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=1336955.579.1.8f19390fe3bb0301be48d16f68bf18cc)
---
We are less than 10 days away from [Sheridan.Church's](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=1336955.580.1.d49e3f23342ca7e15af76442e9780c37) annual conference in Tulsa, OK called [IGNITE 2023](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=1336955.581.1.fb05ea700723aaf05a513ccfdd661b46)!
[IGNITE](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=1336955.582.1.32c39a5816d781058ac248df51f77de3) at [Sheridan.Church](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=1336955.583.1.f47f46e649a80bfc437a903ccb4e3116) is a 4 days of _powerful worship_ , _dynamic speakers_ and a _community of believers on fire for God_.
**Dates** : September 10-13
**Speakers** :
-Lara Trump
-Kash Patel
-Dr. Randy Caldwell
-Bishop Darrell Hines
-David Ring
**Location** : [Sheridan.Church](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=1336955.584.1.effa60bfb26a8fdfa6129b9f3f3d1607) | 7901 E 41st Tulsa, OK 74145
**Cost** : FREE
**RSVP** : [www.sheridan.church/ignite23](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=1336955.585.1.b683847232e9125a16af08bf66f21b0b)
| | [ RSVP: IGNITE 2023 ](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=1336955.586.1.1fc77389b87ab79d9cc4f4d147a4edac)
---
---
## Special Guest Speaker Coming To Sheridan In October
---
| [ ](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=1336955.587.1.71eb89b700cc9e305d6d2bf29e94f053)
---
I have always been a _major_ sports fan.
Recently, I watched a documentary on the great **New York Mets** baseball player **Darryl Strawberry**.
I knew of **Darryl Strawberry's** tremendous career and his unfortunate downfall to drugs later in his career.
What I did not know was how God restored him and called Darryl to the ministry.
Shortly after learning this, I began following **Darryl Strawberry** and his ministry and have been amazed at the preacher he is.
This is why I am excited to invite you to join me at [Sheridan.Church](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=1336955.588.1.3b83b788251c8cd03327e0bb16044421) Tulsa on **Sunday, October 8** to hear **Darryl Strawberry** preach the Gospel!
Thank you,
Jackson Lahmeyer
Follow me on [TruthSocial](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=1336955.589.1.a3c8f63e526cb271713fd558ed46fa95), GAB, [Facebook](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=1336955.590.1.b983d4acaecd18dc3c124eed39815748), [Instagram](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=1336955.591.1.ab0ac43493be074f1d48158db7c82a79), [Twitter](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=1336955.592.1.edec72d9d0f9b5d0891d00eb86012958), [Telegram](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=1336955.593.1.1b70ecc6709ea4f0dcbc7cd4bb5defc2), GETTR & [YouTube](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=1336955.594.1.c6d2e13ebb8fba598a9d484f13250726)).
[www.jacksonlahmeyer.com](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=1336955.595.1.8d49472a6ae4643da43fe0a3d05bf2f3)
| | [ Learn More About Sheridan.Church Tulsa & OKC ](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=1336955.596.1.3fd4c51026e8057e9738d6bf1a2c97bb)

---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/mansharamani/p/panama-drought-creates-global-traffic?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store)
---
| [](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F17fd587d-0523-4c01-815b-e53468247f7d_1600x900.png)|
---|---|---
Last week, I had the opportunity to visit the Panama Canal, one of the most strategic waterways in the Western Hemisphere. It sits at the literal crossroads of North and South America as well as the Pacific and Atlantic oceans. A meaningful percentage of global trade, [worth approximately $270 billion](https://substack.com/redirect/35e0bc4f-5d6b-40c0-8bc5-34843d5e5d31?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), transits the isthmus in Central America each day. [In 2022, more than 14,000 vessels went through the canal, most of which were container ships carrying goods between Asia and the American east coast or Europe](https://substack.com/redirect/ee461769-246e-473d-9b10-a06fb4d5cce1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). And the strategic value of the Panama Canal is substantial, allowing for rapid naval movements that would otherwise be hampered by a lengthy journey around the southern tip of South America.
[](https://mansharamani.substack.com/p/panama-drought-creates-global-traffic#media-8ff5e09a-6a86-4328-be61-89dffd169280)
The economic and strategic value of a waterway connecting the Atlantic and Pacific oceans motivated potential canal builders going back as far back as the 1500s when the Spanish Crown sought to unite both seas. The French made numerous attempts to build a canal in the 1800s, but as the century came to an end, so too did their funding. Further, most attempts to construct the canal led to debilitating disease and illness among the workers.
That's when America was persuaded to abandon a waterway connecting the oceans through Nicaragua. But when Colombia failed to ratify the treaty granting a ten-mile wide stretch across the isthmus to the United States for the construction of a canal, US President Teddy Roosevelt supported Panama's independence from Colombia in 1903, and secured the future of the Panama Canal.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..LjYdSbiHR-bR1NjH_5d17qLa_mDQWgWcXuaLcK1361Q?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=136928388)
Turns out that the key challenge in constructing the canal was worker health, specifically yellow fever and malaria. And it was American doctors that solved the problem by focusing on controlling disease-carrying mosquitos. By 1914, the canal was completed and on August 14th of that year, the _Ancon_ successful navigated the interoceanic waterway that was owned, controlled, and managed by the United States.
| [](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fa2d5c001-22b5-4b89-8d9d-4d2c981cd3f9_2020x1395.jpeg)|
---|---|---
Source: [Panama Canal Museum (https://pcmc.domains.uflib.ufl.edu/object-of-the-day/ss-ancon-august-15-1914/)](https://pcmc.domains.uflib.ufl.edu/object-of-the-day/ss-ancon-august-15-1914/)
Over the next few decades, tensions rose as Panamanians sought a modification of the treaty that granted sovereignty over the Canal Zone to America. These efforts culminated in US President Jimmy Carter agreeing to give full ownership and control of the canal and surrounding lands to Panama in 1999.
The canal is an engineering marvel that enables gigantic ships containing vehicles, petroleum products, bulk commodities, and cruise passengers to rise 26 meters (~85 feet) above sea level in order to cross between the oceans. The design of the canal involved creating a manmade lake at the highest point, which allows gravity to move water into the locks as needed.
| [](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F4e5a4068-6dc7-4d38-8d9b-07118f5e7343_957x614.jpeg)|
---|---|---
Source: <https://www.controlglobal.com/measure/flow/article/11304085/controlling-the-panama-canal>
During my visit to the Miraflores Locks, I watched a gigantic oil tanker get lifted by 26 million gallons of water that flowed in its chamber in about 10 minutes. And here's the part I found fascinating: not one pump is used to move even a drop of water. Gravity does all the work. But as you can see on the schematic above, this is only possible because of the water held in Gatun Lake. On average, [each ship that passes through the canal results in 55 million gallons of water being flushed out to sea](https://substack.com/redirect/b4b2c503-4eb4-4faa-bedb-9a535218fa91?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Each day, [the canal uses three times the water used by New York City](https://substack.com/redirect/10d1146e-ce4b-4ed0-bb0b-d6d7fc619dfc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
One of the concerns I've had for a few years ([See #6 on my list of global predictions for 2022-2027](https://substack.com/redirect/77f86b6a-6bb5-44cb-8aff-c198f9ccfb88?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)) is that canal operations are at risk of disruption if Panama suffers a drought. Unfortunately, that's exactly what's happened. Leaders at the Panama Canal Authority are restricting ship weights and reducing the number of daily crossings in a quest to conserve water. As a result, there's now a traffic jam of vessels waiting to enter the canal.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..LjYdSbiHR-bR1NjH_5d17qLa_mDQWgWcXuaLcK1361Q?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=136928388)
The congestion is now causing ripples throughout global supply chains. A recent _Reuters_ report summarized the issue and its impact:
> _[Ship owners have the options of carrying less cargo, shifting to alternate routes that can add thousands of miles to the trip or grappling with queues that earlier this month backed up 160 vessels and delayed some ships by as much as 21 days. The restrictions already are sending China-U.S. spot shipping prices up as much as 36%...Restrictions at the canal started earlier this year, affecting about 170 countries and virtually every type of good - including soybeans and liquefied natural gas from the United States, copper and fresh cherries from Chile, and beef from Brazil.](https://substack.com/redirect/ee461769-246e-473d-9b10-a06fb4d5cce1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_
Ship owners are getting so desperate that some are paying millions to avoid the congestion. According to _Business Insider_ , "[a $2.4 million price tag was the winning bid for an auction held by the canal authorities for vessels to secure slots to sail through the waterway.](https://substack.com/redirect/723a806a-8c9a-437e-9639-7cd322c00aef?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)"
Of course, insufficient water is not the only potential risk to the Panama Canal. [In 2010, extraordinarily heavy rains caused Gatun Lake to overflow and the flooding that followed forced a closure of the canal as the locks were inundated](https://substack.com/redirect/188310af-8e1f-4b0f-a190-c5a9c3718ea7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). And as we saw in 2021, it's also possible for a ship to get stuck in the canal. In March of that year, the Suez Canal was blocked for six days by the _Ever Given_ , a massive container ship. A mere five days after its grounding, the traffic jam had more than 369 vessels queuing to pass through the canal, [holding up about $10 billion worth of goods each day](https://substack.com/redirect/2bd907ba-5bb4-4eb9-a326-cab0b367b08d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), in a passageway that regularly handles more than 10% of global trade each day. ([Incidentally, a year after this happened, the ](https://substack.com/redirect/297b201d-7332-48ce-b48d-afe35522d65a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[Ever Forward](https://substack.com/redirect/297b201d-7332-48ce-b48d-afe35522d65a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[ grounded itself in Chesapeake Bay](https://substack.com/redirect/297b201d-7332-48ce-b48d-afe35522d65a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).)
[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F7d57f8e7-1887-402f-a542-73170eedbbf5_2500x1578.jpeg)
There's also the obvious military risk associated with strategic bottlenecks. In 1967, the Suez Canal was blocked by Egyptian forces at the onset of the Six-Day War and remain closed until after the Yom Kippur War. It wasn't until after US and British minesweepers cleared the Suez Canal in 1974 that Egypt reopened the canal. Given the strategic importance of the Panama Canal, it's noteworthy that the commander of the Islamic Republic of Iran's navy, Rear Admiral Shahram Irani, stated bluntly "[We are planning to be present in the Panama Canal](https://substack.com/redirect/54d2ef49-62ec-43ae-bcb3-43437017ce80?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)" in January of this year. The Islamic regime, one of the world's most extreme sponsors of terror, then sent two warships to the Western hemisphere, the _Dena_ and the _Makran_. According to the Tehran Times, "[Dena is a Mowj-class warship that...is outfitted with anti-ship cruise missiles, torpedoes and naval cannons. Makran, a forward base ship weighing 121,000 tons, is...used to support the combat vessels logistically and can carry five helicopters](https://substack.com/redirect/08ca14b3-db43-4926-925d-a9a956a4a9b1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)."
The drought in Panama and security risks are also motivating many to reconsider alternatives to the Panama Canal. Going back to the late 1800s, there has always consistent interest in constructing a canal through Nicaragua. In fact, [a Chinese firm suggested it would do so in 2013 and secured a 50 year concession to build the canal ](https://substack.com/redirect/60262277-6cc8-400e-a7cd-bfb95a77e365?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)at an estimated cost of ~$40 billion. And just last year during his Independence Day speech, Nicaraguan President Daniel Ortega noted that "[the canal will be a reality at some point](https://substack.com/redirect/60262277-6cc8-400e-a7cd-bfb95a77e365?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)." ([I'm not holding my breath, see #13 on my list of global predictions for 2015-2020](https://substack.com/redirect/f0f2b76d-a82a-46e5-b788-6dd744a907bb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).)
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..LjYdSbiHR-bR1NjH_5d17qLa_mDQWgWcXuaLcK1361Q?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=136928388)
Another option that has been kicked around since the 1970s is the construction of a huge interconnected network of railways, highways, and pipelines to create a land bridge across the Isthmus of Tehuantepec in Southern Mexico. [President Andres Manuel Lopez Obrador almost tripled funding for the project in 2024 and has suggested it could compete with the Panama Canal](https://substack.com/redirect/7ee2ff8d-b80f-4049-a83f-620e6ccf188c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
And late last year, Florida-based [Zergatran](https://substack.com/redirect/c905ef17-ca40-44a6-90ce-0d957ef2ee20?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) proposed a green shipping corridor in Colombia that would use an [underground maglev train and automated ports to transfer containers between the oceans in less than 30 minutes](https://substack.com/redirect/35e0bc4f-5d6b-40c0-8bc5-34843d5e5d31?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) via route in the far north of Colombia. [The company is in the midst of trying to raise money for the project](https://substack.com/redirect/1717afcd-0536-461c-b1e5-9206e58680c6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and offers a futuristic vision that has yet to get much attention.
[](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=f5gPOXxolRE)
While it's unclear how these dynamics will ultimately play out, I continue to watch closely for the impact they may have on the global economy. If this turns out to be a short term blip, then we may not notice any meaningful impact. If, however, congestion continues or additional disruptions surface, it's worth paying attention to the following potential ramifications:
1. The rising tensions and risks in the Panama Canal provide further incentives for global manufacturing to **shorten supply chains** , and to shift them towards friendly territories. The _[friendshoring](https://substack.com/redirect/8ae73fb4-4c3e-49f0-baaa-46ae2a5db40d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[ trend remains in tact](https://substack.com/redirect/8ae73fb4-4c3e-49f0-baaa-46ae2a5db40d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and may in fact accelerate if canal disruptions increase. Mexico may continue to be benefit as the manufacturers seek to move operations closer to US markets. And supply chain strategies will continue to shift away from just-in-time approaches to ones that adopt a just-in-case logic. Resilience will trump efficiency as a criteria for strategy
2. The ripples from congestion in the Panama Canal may **create** **port** **disruptions** as shippers adjust routes. While container ships have been least affected to date, the longer the disruption continues, the more likely it becomes that goods are sent to West Coast ports instead of East Coast ones. [The global transportation system is not designed to dynamically handle surging and shrinking demand and this could wreak havoc on trucking and rails industries](https://substack.com/redirect/c9b23ff9-1bed-48a8-b457-459f97f3cf2d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
3. Incrementally, these dynamics point to **sustained inflationary pressure** with higher costs to transport goods that should make their way into consumer prices. While it's unclear how severe the price pressure will be, a sustained canal disruption that leads to higher costs might drive central banks around the world to continue raising interest rates, compounding global economic chaos in unpredictable ways.

Dr. Mansharamani is a global generalist who tries to look beyond the short term view that tends to dominate today's agenda. He spends his time speaking with leaders in business, government, academia, and journalism...and prides himself on voraciously consuming a wide variety of books, magazines, articles, TV shows, and podcasts. _LinkedIn_ twice listed him as their #1 Top Voice for Global Economics and _Worth_ profiled him on their list of the 100 Most Powerful People in Global Finance. Sustained congestion at the Panama Canal could ripple through global supply chains | | | Sep 11 | | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- Last week, I had the opportunity to visit the Panama Canal, one of the most strategic waterways in the Western Hemisphere. It sits at the literal crossroads of North and South America as well as the Pacific and Atlantic oceans. A meaningful percentage of global trade, , transits the isthmus in Central America each day. . And the strategic value of the Panama Canal is substantial, allowing for rapid naval movements that would otherwise be hampered by a lengthy journey around the southern tip of South America. The economic and strategic value of a waterway connecting the Atlantic and Pacific oceans motivated potential canal builders going back as far back as the 1500s when the Spanish Crown sought to unite both seas. The French made numerous attempts to build a canal in the 1800s, but as the century came to an end, so too did their funding. Further, most attempts to construct the canal led to debilitating disease and illness among the workers. That's when America was persuaded to abandon a waterway connecting the oceans through Nicaragua. But when Colombia failed to ratify the treaty granting a ten-mile wide stretch across the isthmus to the United States for the construction of a canal, US President Teddy Roosevelt supported Panama's independence from Colombia in 1903, and secured the future of the Panama Canal. Turns out that the key challenge in constructing the canal was worker health, specifically yellow fever and malaria. And it was American doctors that solved the problem by focusing on controlling disease-carrying mosquitos. By 1914, the canal was completed and on August 14th of that year, the Ancon successful navigated the interoceanic waterway that was owned, controlled, and managed by the United States. | | ---|---|--- Source: Over the next few decades, tensions rose as Panamanians sought a modification of the treaty that granted sovereignty over the Canal Zone to America. These efforts culminated in US President Jimmy Carter agreeing to give full ownership and control of the canal and surrounding lands to Panama in 1999. The canal is an engineering marvel that enables gigantic ships containing vehicles, petroleum products, bulk commodities, and cruise passengers to rise 26 meters (~85 feet) above sea level in order to cross between the oceans. The design of the canal involved creating a manmade lake at the highest point, which allows gravity to move water into the locks as needed. | | ---|---|--- Source: <> During my visit to the Miraflores Locks, I watched a gigantic oil tanker get lifted by 26 million gallons of water that flowed in its chamber in about 10 minutes. And here's the part I found fascinating: not one pump is used to move even a drop of water. Gravity does all the work. But as you can see on the schematic above, this is only possible because of the water held in Gatun Lake. On average, . Each day, . One of the concerns I've had for a few years () is that canal operations are at risk of disruption if Panama suffers a drought. Unfortunately, that's exactly what's happened. Leaders at the Panama Canal Authority are restricting ship weights and reducing the number of daily crossings in a quest to conserve water. As a result, there's now a traffic jam of vessels waiting to enter the canal. The congestion is now causing ripples throughout global supply chains. A recent Reuters report summarized the issue and its impact: Ship owners are getting so desperate that some are paying millions to avoid the congestion. According to Business Insider , "" Of course, insufficient water is not the only potential risk to the Panama Canal. . And as we saw in 2021, it's also possible for a ship to get stuck in the canal. In March of that year, the Suez Canal was blocked for six days by the Ever Given , a massive container ship. A mere five days after its grounding, the traffic jam had more than 369 vessels queuing to pass through the canal, , in a passageway that regularly handles more than 10% of global trade each day. (.) There's also the obvious military risk associated with strategic bottlenecks. In 1967, the Suez Canal was blocked by Egyptian forces at the onset of the Six-Day War and remain closed until after the Yom Kippur War. It wasn't until after US and British minesweepers cleared the Suez Canal in 1974 that Egypt reopened the canal. Given the strategic importance of the Panama Canal, it's noteworthy that the commander of the Islamic Republic of Iran's navy, Rear Admiral Shahram Irani, stated bluntly "" in January of this year. The Islamic regime, one of the world's most extreme sponsors of terror, then sent two warships to the Western hemisphere, the Dena and the Makran. According to the Tehran Times, "." The drought in Panama and security risks are also motivating many to reconsider alternatives to the Panama Canal. Going back to the late 1800s, there has always consistent interest in constructing a canal through Nicaragua. In fact, an estimated cost of ~$40 billion. And just last year during his Independence Day speech, Nicaraguan President Daniel Ortega noted that "." (.) Another option that has been kicked around since the 1970s is the construction of a huge interconnected network of railways, highways, and pipelines to create a land bridge across the Isthmus of Tehuantepec in Southern Mexico. . And late last year, Florida-based proposed a green shipping corridor in Colombia that would use an via route in the far north of Colombia. , and offers a futuristic vision that has yet to get much attention. While it's unclear how these dynamics will ultimately play out, I continue to watch closely for the impact they may have on the global economy. If this turns out to be a short term blip, then we may not notice any meaningful impact. If, however, congestion continues or additional disruptions surface, it's worth paying attention to the following potential ramifications: 1. The rising tensions and risks in the Panama Canal provide further incentives for global manufacturing to shorten supply chains , and to shift them towards friendly territories. The , and may in fact accelerate if canal disruptions increase. Mexico may continue to be benefit as the manufacturers seek to move operations closer to US markets. And supply chain strategies will continue to shift away from just-in-time approaches to ones that adopt a just-in-case logic. Resilience will trump efficiency as a criteria for strategy 2. The ripples from congestion in the Panama Canal may create port disruptions as shippers adjust routes. While container ships have been least affected to date, the longer the disruption continues, the more likely it becomes that goods are sent to West Coast ports instead of East Coast ones. . 3. Incrementally, these dynamics point to sustained inflationary pressure with higher costs to transport goods that should make their way into consumer prices. While it's unclear how severe the price pressure will be, a sustained canal disruption that leads to higher costs might drive central banks around the world to continue raising interest rates, compounding global economic chaos in unpredictable ways. About Dr. Mansharamani is a global generalist who tries to look beyond the short term view that tends to dominate today's agenda. He spends his time speaking with leaders in business, government, academia, and journalism...and prides himself on voraciously consuming a wide variety of books, magazines, articles, TV shows, and podcasts. LinkedIn twice listed him as their #1 Top Voice for Global Economics and Worth profiled him on their list of the 100 Most Powerful People in Global Finance. He has taught at Yale and Harvard and has a PhD and two masters degrees from MIT. He is also the author of as well as . Follow him on or . Navigating Uncertainty is a reader-supported publication. subject: Judicial Watch: Records Show Biden Justice Department Set Up Unprecedented Apparatus to Target January 6 Protestors
body:
Judicial Watch announced today it received 90 pages of records from the Executive Office for United States Attorneys, a component of the Department of Justice, in a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) lawsuit that detail the extensive apparatus the Biden Justice Department set up to investigate and prosecute January 6 protestors.’’

name: Dr. Tre Pennie
subject: Dr. Tre Pennie Campaign Kick-off
body:
Dear Friends and Supporters,

I am excited to invite you to join me and renowned Wildlife Conservationist
Kristin Navarro on October 5th for the official Pennie for Congress campaign
kick-off event.

<https://list2.trepennie.com/c/25/2/12/4>

This event will provide us an excellent opportunity to come together to
discuss the critical issues impacting our country and how we can collaborate
to address them. Your insights and perspectives are invaluable, and I
believe that by working together, we can make a positive difference in our
community and beyond.

Date: October 5th, 2023

Time: 6:30pm

Location: Del Frisco's Double Eagle Steakhouse (Plano Legacy West), 5905
Legacy Dr, Suite A120, Plano, TX 75024

Cost: $95

Note: If you have already supported the campaign, your event admission has
been recorded.

<https://list2.trepennie.com/c/25/2/12/1>
Pay Here

<https://list2.trepennie.com/c/25/2/12/1>

Dr. Tre Pennie

U.S. House Candidate

Texas Congressional District 3

www.pennieforcongress.com <https://list2.trepennie.com/c/25/2/12/5>

If you have any questions or concerns, please contact us at: (469) 493-8950
or email us at: info@pennieforcongress.com
<mailto:info@pennieforcongress.com>

##################################################
Unsubscribe / Manage Email Preferences:
https://list2.trepennie.com/r/2/dpwillis67%40gmail.com

body:
What you need to know today. No images? [Click here](https://caucusroom.cmail19.com/t/i-e-ajyyhjt-trhiujldw-w/) [](https://caucusroom.cmail19.com/t/i-l-ajyyhjt-trhiujldw-r/)
## **It's Monday! It's the anniversary of 9/11.[_Biden is ignoring it._](https://caucusroom.cmail19.com/t/i-l-ajyyhjt-trhiujldw-y/)**
[Poll Results: Which Group Are You In?](https://caucusroom.cmail19.com/t/i-l-ajyyhjt-trhiujldw-j/) **Gov of New Mexico Suspends 2nd ****Amendment** The Gov of New Mexico **[made a shocking public health order](https://caucusroom.cmail19.com/t/i-l-ajyyhjt-trhiujldw-t/)** , and even **[anti-gun activists](https://caucusroom.cmail19.com/t/i-l-ajyyhjt-trhiujldw-i/)** are speaking out. As a result, an **[emergency lawsuit](https://caucusroom.cmail19.com/t/i-l-ajyyhjt-trhiujldw-d/)** has been filed. **Two Can Play at This Game** Georgia's Republican AG announces **[indictment of 61 leftists under the RICO statute](https://caucusroom.cmail19.com/t/i-l-ajyyhjt-trhiujldw-h/)** used just weeks ago against Trump. **Making a Bad Situation Worse** Campaign finance documents found a connection between **[Liberty Safe's parent company and the Democratic Party](https://caucusroom.cmail19.com/t/i-l-ajyyhjt-trhiujldw-k/)** in an attempt to restrict American's Second Amendment rights. **Isn't That Already in June?** This crazy state has decided to **[recognize a new group](https://caucusroom.cmail19.com/t/i-l-ajyyhjt-trhiujldw-u/) **for history month.

**Youngkin Pardon** Virginia Gov. **[Youngkin has pardoned a Loudoun County father who protested against the sexual assault of his daughter](https://caucusroom.cmail19.com/t/i-l-ajyyhjt-trhiujldw-p/)** and what he perceived as the public school's attempt to cover up the incident. [_**Let**_](https://caucusroom.cmail19.com/t/i-l-ajyyhjt-trhiujldw-x/)**[ _'s _](https://caucusroom.cmail19.com/t/i-l-ajyyhjt-trhiujldw-m/)[have a good laugh](https://caucusroom.cmail19.com/t/i-l-ajyyhjt-trhiujldw-c/)! **😆
* How to tell if **[you are old](https://caucusroom.cmail19.com/t/i-l-ajyyhjt-trhiujldw-q/)**.
* **[Coming soon](https://caucusroom.cmail19.com/t/i-l-ajyyhjt-trhiujldw-a/)** to the campaign trail.
* He's **[confused](https://caucusroom.cmail19.com/t/i-l-ajyyhjt-trhiujldw-f/)**.
* Come and **[get it](https://caucusroom.cmail19.com/t/i-l-ajyyhjt-trhiujldw-z/)**.

**Countdown to the 2nd Republican Primary Debate!** [](https://caucusroom.cmail19.com/t/i-l-ajyyhjt-trhiujldw-v/)[](https://caucusroom.cmail19.com/t/i-l-ajyyhjt-trhiujldw-e/)[](https://caucusroom.cmail19.com/t/i-l-ajyyhjt-trhiujldw-s/) [](https://caucusroom.cmail19.com/t/i-l-ajyyhjt-trhiujldw-g/) | CaucusRoom, LLC

name: Joe Kerr
subject: A tragic day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Joe Kerr for Congress
body:
---
| | Peter,

On September 11, 2001, our country experienced one of the greatest tragedies in American history.

Thousands of lives were lost, including 343 firefighters. The brave firefighters who ran into the towers on that tragic day are heroes, and their work and their lives should be remembered and honored. In the wake of 9/11, I led the effort to add the American flag to each of our OCFA fire engines as a rolling reminder of 9/11 and the sacrifices made — one of the first efforts of its kind made across the country.

As a second-generation firefighter, I know that every day on the job is risky. We’re running inside burning buildings, putting out fires, responding to disasters, and saving lives.

However, what may come as a surprise to many, is that cancer is the number one killer of firefighters. Three-fourths of firefighters in Orange County and across the country are contracting cancer at an alarming rate.

A startling statistic: After 9/11, more firefighters who were exposed to toxins and carcinogens while working on The Pile during rescue and recovery efforts have died from cancer than those who perished during the attacks.

The September 11th Victim Compensation Fund, which provides critical funding to the families of people who died or were injured on 9/11, is running out.

**It’s up to Congress to make sure 9/11 responders and their families get the proper healthcare, benefits, and funding for their critical needs.** [Sign on now to join me in calling on Congress to act now for 9/11 responders:](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69577520/426576937/832161463?refcode=JK_EM_PT_90DO_230911_1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDc4NjEiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNWZhZWNhMjEtZWY1MC1lZTExLWEzZjEtMDAyMjQ4MzJlMWJhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=NO9y8AYZu9n815WWvhNHWxzbkhY96MS4OS1kDK1Ii2o=&emci=7d7cbd15-ef50-ee11-a3f1-00224832e1ba&emdi=5faeca21-ef50-ee11-a3f1-00224832e1ba&ceid=8978519) | | **[SIGN ON](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69577521/426576938/832161463?refcode=JK_EM_PT_90DO_230911_1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDc4NjEiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNWZhZWNhMjEtZWY1MC1lZTExLWEzZjEtMDAyMjQ4MzJlMWJhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=NO9y8AYZu9n815WWvhNHWxzbkhY96MS4OS1kDK1Ii2o=&emci=7d7cbd15-ef50-ee11-a3f1-00224832e1ba&emdi=5faeca21-ef50-ee11-a3f1-00224832e1ba&ceid=8978519)**

name: Dr. Shawn Wilson for Governor
subject: Debating the issues
body:
image of ... [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP50/1/104399/images/swlogo2.png]
TEAM WILSON WEEKLY UPDATES
Peter,
I was grateful for the chance to discuss the issues with most of the other candidates in the field at this Thursday’s debate - showing a statewide television and streaming audience what Team Wilson is all about.
Two things were clear from the debate.
I’m the only candidate who came prepared with 25 years of experience working with both Democrats and Republicans.
And our state’s top law enforcement officer was too scared to face voters.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GgQlG0PpTdM [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GgQlG0PpTdM]
CHIP IN [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sw-web]
HIGHLIGHTS FROM THE CAMPAIGN TRAIL
I was honored to deliver keynote remarks to the Prince Hall Freemasons this week. A historically Black organization committed to the ideals of liberty, equality and peace, I am grateful to share common goals with their members.
Their spirit of breaking barriers and lifting up the community through collective partnerships is exactly the energy we need on October 14th!
wilsonforla.com [wilsonforla.com]
New Orleans
Feeling grateful for the opportunity to join Big Shot Shane Williams at Zulu Social Aid & Pleasure Club .
Having grown up in Algiers, Shane and his family were stand-out citizens, always putting community first! I had such a great time celebrating his title of Big Shot for this year’s Mardi Gras festivities!
wilsonforla.com [wilsonforla.com]
Opelousas
This past weekend #TeamWilson was down in Southwest LA for the Zydeco Music Festival .
We had a fantastic time meeting with supporters from near and far. True to Louisiana, our connections were made better by sharing in great music and great food!
wilsonforla.com [wilsonforla.com]
East Feliciana
This weekend, our East Feliciana for Wilson crew was out at the Clinton Louisiana Farmers Market handing out push cards and fans to attendees. Be on the lookout because #TeamWilson is EVERYWHERE spreading the word about our campaign!
Head to wilsonforla.com [http://wilsonforla.com] to learn more about why I'm the best candidate in this race to move Louisiana forward!
wilsonforla.com [wilsonforla.com]
ENDORSEMENTS
State Representatives Randal L. Gaines, Ed Larvadain, III, & Pat Moore
The Wilson Wave continues to grow, with three more State Representatives announcing their endorsement.
Thank you to State Reps Randal L. Gaines, Ed Larvadain III, and Pat Moore.
WILSONFORLA.COM [WILSONFORLA.COM]
United Teachers of New Orleans
I am proudly endorsed by the United Teachers of New Orleans , a leg of the Louisiana Federation of Teachers and School Employees , American Federation of Teachers , and AFL-CIO .
They know as Governor I will work to ensure that our teachers are adequately compensated so they can provide high quality education to our students.
wilsoforla.com [wilsoforla.com]
HOST SHAWN AT AN EVENT [events@wilsonforla.com?subject=Host%20Shawn%20At%20An%20Event]
To learn more about my candidacy visit wilsonforla.com [wilsonforla.com] .
With hope,
Shawn Wilson
DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sw-web]
ENDORSE [https://www.wilsonforla.com/endorse]
LEARN MORE [WILSONFORLA.COM]
https://www.facebook.com/ [https://www.facebook.com/] https://twitter.com/ [https://twitter.com/] https://instagram.com/ [https://instagram.com/] https://www.linkedin.com/ [https://www.linkedin.com/]

Wilson for Louisiana
P.O. Box 4572
Baton Rouge, LA 70821
United States

name: Joseph Geevarghese @ Our Revolution
subject: Solidarity with Autoworkers
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Our Revolution click here to unsubscribe
body:
[ stdin# ][IMG]

Peter Willis, this week, could embark on one of the BIGGEST strikes of the year, and we want to show them we have their back!

Sign here so autoworkers will see your name and know that a powerful progressive movement stands with them as they take on this massive fight for what they deserve against the corporate bosses. (https://act.ourrevolution.com/sign/uaw-khanna-pt-2309/?source=EM_NBI_OR_20230911_PT_uaw_0_3MO_jg_na_na_uaw&t=1011&akid=4660%2E5885482%2EmCBp8-)

In 2007-2009, the Great Recession turned the auto industry upside down. To save it, autoworkers took massive cuts to their wages and benefits when the companies introduced “tiers,” worse pay for the same work. Pensions were eliminated. Post-retirement healthcare vanished for new hires. Jobs were cut.

In short, the companies got billions in taxpayer bailout dollars, while autoworkers took deep cuts and made life-changing sacrifices to keep the industry alive.

Flash forward to 2023: Autoworkers built $250,000,000,000 in North American profits for the Big Three in the past decade — that’s a quarter of a TRILLION dollars. CEO pay is up 40% in four years; autoworker wages increased only 6% in that time, while inflation is up 18%.

Now, as their contract is set to expire, the United Auto Workers (UAW) union has been in negotiations with General Motors, Ford, and Stellantis, making reasonable but necessary demands:
* Fair pay increases
* Improved benefits and Pensions
* An end to the tiered wage system
* And job security for all workers during the transition to electric vehicle

Peter Willis, you see what’s at stake. That’s why, this past weekend, I joined UAW President Sean Fain and US Rep. Rashida Tlaib for a virtual rally and saw the solidarity of these workers first hand. And, they’re not just fighting for one another, they’re advancing the fight for workers all across the country.

We need our government to be on the side of workers, and not greedy bosses who get bailouts from taxpayers and then cut pay and benefits for their workers.

The last few years have been monumental for a resurgent labor movement in America, and as progressives we are proud to help build worker power, join picket lines, and lend our voices to amplify these critical battles.

Take a stand right now and add your name to the millions speaking out to stand with United Autoworkers and their families as they get ready to take this fight to the next level. (https://act.ourrevolution.com/sign/uaw-khanna-pt-2309/?source=EM_NBI_OR_20230911_PT_uaw_0_3MO_jg_na_na_uaw&t=1012&akid=4660%2E5885482%2EmCBp8-)

When we organize, we win!

Joseph Geevarghese
Executive Director, Our Revolution

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

subject: Stepping into September
body:
The Season of Change | | | | | [](https://warriormom.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7c0b21e245e40197b3670bea2&id=e0bb48c8f1&e=3a42edfdc4)
---
Temperature drops are around the corner and the days will shorten in due time. As the leaves start to fall, soak up the final moments outdoors before it gets too cold. **Grab your copy of**[**Warrior Mom: A Mother’s Journey in Healing Her Son with Autism**](https://warriormom.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7c0b21e245e40197b3670bea2&id=c647399e4b&e=3a42edfdc4) and find a cozy spot outside for reading!
**Purchase**[**Warrior Mom**](https://warriormom.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7c0b21e245e40197b3670bea2&id=988eac7398&e=3a42edfdc4)**today wherever books are sold.**

name: Conor O'Callaghan
subject: Remembering September 11, 2001
disclaimer_text: Paid for and authorized by Conor for AZ
body:
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69577854/426578231/993309821?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDc0NDIiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMmFmYzZjZGItZjM1MC1lZTExLWEzZjEtMDAyMjQ4MzJlMWJhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Xr6PYHUHYGbN0TrvJ_-zTtPWROxgX5AJF98OT2Nlctw=&emci=4ddd4312-bf50-ee11-a3f1-00224832e1ba&emdi=2afc6cdb-f350-ee11-a3f1-00224832e1ba&ceid=7520491)
---

| | On this anniversary of September 11, 2001, I reflect on the profound impact that day had on our nation and my own life. 22 years ago today, as I was just beginning my college journey in Philadelphia, the terrorist attacks cast a shadow over our country. I watched in disbelief as the towering symbols of American strength crumbled into ruins, the Pentagon was engulfed in flames, and the first images appeared of an ominous crater in Shanksville, Pennsylvania. From the falling man to the evacuation of Congress to the heart-wrenching images of the missing on our screens, that day forever changed us as Americans and altered human history.

As I began my career in finance and met my wife, Tina, shortly thereafter, the enduring impact of 9/11 cast a persistent somberness over the place where we worked. It influenced business practices, added gravity to fire drills in Manhattan skyscrapers, and undoubtedly affected how people gazed at the sky during breaks. We were consistently reminded of lives tragically lost on the very streets where industry and normalcy were still striving to recover in the mid-2000s. As immigrants, we grappled not only with the fear of another attack, but also with disheartening prejudices faced by people who resembled my wife.

For those old enough to remember, the impact of that day remains etched in our minds. Yet, amid the chaos and pain, we still feel the strength that emerged when we discovered unity and shared determination born out of tragedy—a testament to the unwavering American spirit. This spirit defines the America I cherish—a nation that unites in crisis, transcending our differences.

From those who lived through it to new generations just now learning about it, let's ensure this legacy endures—a legacy of courage, compassion, and an unbreakable bond. United, there's no challenge too great.

As we honor those we lost and the heroes who emerged, let's rekindle that unity and determination. In doing so, we honor our nation's resilience, the memories of those taken, and commit to a brighter future together as one America.

_Conor_ |
---|---|---

| | | Paid for and authorized by Conor for AZ.
---

clean_body: | | | | On this anniversary of September 11, 2001, I reflect on the profound impact that day had on our nation and my own life. 22 years ago today, as I was just beginning my college journey in Philadelphia, the terrorist attacks cast a shadow over our country. I watched in disbelief as the towering symbols of American strength crumbled into ruins, the Pentagon was engulfed in flames, and the first images appeared of an ominous crater in Shanksville, Pennsylvania. From the falling man to the evacuation of Congress to the heart-wrenching images of the missing on our screens, that day forever changed us as Americans and altered human history. As I began my career in finance and met my wife, Tina, shortly thereafter, the enduring impact of 9/11 cast a persistent somberness over the place where we worked. It influenced business practices, added gravity to fire drills in Manhattan skyscrapers, and undoubtedly affected how people gazed at the sky during breaks. We were consistently reminded of lives tragically lost on the very streets where industry and normalcy were still striving to recover in the mid-2000s. As immigrants, we grappled not only with the fear of another attack, but also with disheartening prejudices faced by people who resembled my wife. For those old enough to remember, the impact of that day remains etched in our minds. Yet, amid the chaos and pain, we still feel the strength that emerged when we discovered unity and shared determination born out of tragedy—a testament to the unwavering American spirit. This spirit defines the America I cherish—a nation that unites in crisis, transcending our differences. From those who lived through it to new generations just now learning about it, let's ensure this legacy endures—a legacy of courage, compassion, and an unbreakable bond. United, there's no challenge too great. As we honor those we lost and the heroes who emerged, let's rekindle that unity and determination. In doing so, we honor our nation's resilience, the memories of those taken, and commit to a brighter future together as one America. Conor | ---|---|--- | | | Paid for and authorized by Conor for AZ. |

name: HUGE NEWS (via Adam for Colorado)
subject: Lauren Boebert panicking
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Adam for Colorado

Last year, Adam Frisch came just 546 votes short of defeating Boebert. It was the closest race in the country. And now, after a new poll shows a TIED race, all the political pundits are saying that Adam can finish the job and win in 2024.

name: Hilary Franz
subject: 22 years ago today
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Hilary Franz (D)
body:
Today, I am thinking of the nearly 3,000 victims and their families and also remembering the heroism of the firefighters and first responders who risked their lives to save others.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69578592/426581282/2121138121?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDA1NzQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNzBkNjU2MTQtZjg1MC1lZTExLWEzZjEtMDAyMjQ4MzJlMWJhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=x1K-FDsBkLWs5fRkFh8ScwOaLzvthinDxLDJI1CBwf8=&emci=6dd65614-f850-ee11-a3f1-00224832e1ba&emdi=70d65614-f850-ee11-a3f1-00224832e1ba&ceid=11003312)
---
| Peter,

It has been 22 years since the tragedy of September 11th – it will always remain a day of sorrow and reflection.

Today, I am thinking of the nearly 3,000 victims and their families and also remembering the heroism of the firefighters and first responders who risked their lives to save others.

In the face of unimaginable adversity, the unity and resilience of our nation shone through. We came together to support one another and rebuild. It serves as a reminder that in our darkest moments, we can find strength, hope, and courage.

Just this weekend, I was in Medical Lake, visiting those who are recovering from the Gray Fire. In the midst of that wildfire, the heroism and selflessness of our firefighters and first responders shone bright.

While there, I met with a firefighter whose own home was lost in the blaze while he fought to protect the homes of others. And if he had to do it again, he would – an enduring reminder that the heroism of 9/11 has not dimmed.

Take a moment today to reflect on the importance of unity, compassion, and the enduring spirit of America.

With deepest respect and remembrance,

– Hilary Franz
---

* * *
| | [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69578593/426581283/2121138121?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDA1NzQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNzBkNjU2MTQtZjg1MC1lZTExLWEzZjEtMDAyMjQ4MzJlMWJhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=x1K-FDsBkLWs5fRkFh8ScwOaLzvthinDxLDJI1CBwf8=&emci=6dd65614-f850-ee11-a3f1-00224832e1ba&emdi=70d65614-f850-ee11-a3f1-00224832e1ba&ceid=11003312)

name: Team Katie Porter
subject: The only candidate in this race who doesn't take lobbyist money
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Senate
body:
Katie is one of eleven Members who rejects federal lobbyist money.
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/14558?t=1&akid=29704%2E3422442%2EwpQrR0)
**Katie Porter is one of just eleven Members of Congress who rejects federal lobbyist money, and she’s the only candidate in this race to do so.**
Meanwhile...
* In 2022, the oil and gas industry spent **$124.4 million** lobbying the federal government.
* PhRMA, a group that represents pharmaceutical companies, spent **$29.2 million** on federal lobbying last year.
* In just the first half of this year, financial firms and groups representing multinational corporations spent more than **$1 million** lobbying against the corporate minimum tax that was signed into law last year and is supposed to take effect soon.

The power of special interests is at an all time high, and we need leaders like Katie Porter who are willing to call out their BS, reject their checks, and actually fight for working people.
We’ll win this race without a dime from corporate PACs, federal lobbyists, or Big Oil, Big Pharma, or Wall Street CEOs. That means we count on supporters like you: [**Make a $5 donation today to help send Katie Porter to the U.S. Senate to fight against corporate special interests that are rigging the rules against working people.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-20230911-2?refcode=EMAL11_2_LOB_20230911_V1_F&amount=5&refcode2=29704_3422442_wpQrR0&akid=29704%2E3422442%2EwpQrR0)
[Donate $5 ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-20230911-2?refcode=EMAL11_2_LOB_20230911_V1_F&amount=5&refcode2=29704_3422442_wpQrR0&akid=29704%2E3422442%2EwpQrR0)
Thanks so much for being a part of our campaign.
Team KP

Katie is running for the U.S. Senate because Californians deserve a warrior fighting for them in Washington. Our campaign is people-powered, we don’t take a dime from corporate PACs, federal lobbyists, or executives from Big Oil or Big Pharma. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-footer?refcode=EMAL11_2_LOB_20230911_V1_F&t=2&refcode2=29704_3422442_wpQrR0&akid=29704%2E3422442%2EwpQrR0)**
